findings.title: projectSkills priority never wired through from buildFidelityAddition
findings.description: selectSkills supports a projectSkills[] param to prioritize project-local skills (AC-US4-01: 'sw:* + project skills first'), but buildFidelityAddition calls selectSkills without passing projectSkills, so it always defaults to []. Only sw:* prioritization is active. Acceptable: the proxy layer has no reliable project-membership signal for harvested skills, and sw:* (the workflow-critical tier) IS prioritized. AC-US4-01's core requirement (bounded index, sw:* first, names-only degradation) is met and tested. Documented here for future improvement, not blocking.

findings.title: selectSkills accepts fidelity param but does not use it
findings.description: selectSkills destructures `fidelity = 'balanced'` but never references it. The full-tier behavior (keep whenToUse, higher clamp) is handled entirely upstream in buildFidelityAddition via descChars/keepWhenToUse before harvesting. The param is dead. Harmless but slightly misleading API surface.

findings.title: Prefix-cache gate widened to all local providers — verified response-path-safe
findings.description: The gate `if (provider.name === 'ollama')` was widened to `if (isLocal)`, so lmstudio/llamacpp now flow through getOrStore and receive a non-null prefixCacheResult passed to transformResponse (proxy.mjs:920) and createStreamTranslator (proxy.mjs:930). Verified safe: lmstudio/llamacpp delegate to openai-local.mjs, whose transformResponse:(body)=>translateResponse(body,...) and createStreamTranslator:()=>createStreamTranslator(...) structurally discard the extra arg. No null-deref or metrics path is exercised for these providers. Integration test 'prefix-cache now covers the LM Studio (MLX) path without breaking it' confirms a successful 200 + injected catalog. This is the highest-risk change in the increment and it is sound.
